quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungarian paprika isn't just delicious—it was so valuable that it was used as currency in medieval Hungary! The spice comes in eight different varieties, from sweet and mild to fiery hot. Today, the town of Szeged is famous worldwide for producing some of the finest paprika.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals in the Strait of Juan de Fuca have fascinating sleeping habits—they can sleep underwater and surface to breathe without even waking up! Learn about these charming neighbors and how they've adapted to life in the Pacific Northwest.

medium_read.title: The Wildflower Super Bloom of Olympic National Park
medium_read.teaser: July is peak wildflower season in the Olympic Mountains, painting the alpine meadows in brilliant colors. From lupines to Indian paintbrush, discover which flowers are blooming right now and the best trails to see them.

long_read.title: The Immigrant Ships That Built America
long_read.teaser: Between 1880 and 1924, over 20 million immigrants sailed to America seeking new lives, carrying precious family treasures and precious little else. This beautifully illustrated piece traces their journeys across the Atlantic, including stories from Hungarian families who made the brave crossing.

port_angeles.title: Bald Eagles Thriving in the Strait
port_angeles.description: The Strait of Juan de Fuca is home to one of the highest concentrations of bald eagles in the lower 48 states! Summer is an excellent time to spot them fishing along the shoreline, especially near Ediz Hook. These magnificent birds mate for life and can live over 30 years in the wild.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while in flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's right, Barbara! An incredible 1,200 beats per minute—about 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! A hummingbird's tiny heart beats about 1,200 times per minute when flying—faster than you can imagine!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Actually, their little hearts beat an amazing 1,200 times per minute! That's what powers their incredible hovering ability.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the fastest metabolism of any animal on Earth. To fuel their rapid wingbeats (up to 80 times per second), they must eat about half their body weight in nectar every day!

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'thank you'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Köszönöm! That's exactly right! Such a beautiful word for expressing gratitude.
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The word is 'köszönöm'! It's one of the most important and warm words in Hungarian. 'Szia' means hello, 'igen' means yes, and 'szeretlek' means I love you.
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language—it's part of the Finno-Ugric family, making it more closely related to Finnish and Estonian than to neighboring Slavic or Germanic languages. It's known for its beautiful complexity and 14 vowel sounds!

puzzle_3.question: What special feature do Olympic marmots have that helps them survive long mountain winters?
options: They hibernate for up to 8 months
They store food in underground chambers
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes! Olympic marmots hibernate for up to 8 months—one of the longest hibernations of any mammal. They're found only in the Olympic Mountains and nowhere else on Earth!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Actually, these adorable creatures hibernate for up to 8 months each year! Olympic marmots are found only in the Olympic Mountains, making them one of the region's most special residents.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Olympic marmots are one of the most social members of the squirrel family. They greet each other by touching noses and can often be seen sunbathing on rocks during the brief alpine summer, building up fat reserves for their long winter sleep.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
